Business Category: Vuori operates in the athleisure market, offering premium performance apparel that blends fitness, surf, sport, and art, inspired by the active Coastal California lifestyle. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241108508233/en/Vuori-Announces-825-Million-Investment-Led-by-General-Atlantic-and-Stripes/))
Ownership Details: Vuori, founded by Joe Kudla in 2015, has received significant investments from firms like SoftBank Vision Fund 2, General Atlantic, and Stripes, yet Kudla remains the majority owner. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vuori))
Main Manufacturing: Vuori's sportswear is primarily manufactured in Colombia, Vietnam, and China. ([allamerican.org](https://allamerican.org/investigation/vuori/))
Environmental & Sustainability Policies: Vuori is certified as a Climate Neutral brand, measuring and offsetting its greenhouse gas emissions, with a 2024 footprint of 115,667 tCO₂e, primarily from its supply chain. The company aims to reduce Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 42% and Scope 3 emissions per unit by 48% by 2030, utilizing clean energy, low-carbon materials, and supplier engagement. ([commonshare.com](https://www.commonshare.com/suppliers/vuori)) Vuori also enforces a Vendor Code of Conduct based on International Labour Organization standards, prohibiting child labor, forced labor, wage violations, and environmental negligence. ([commonshare.com](https://www.commonshare.com/suppliers/vuori))
Acquisition History:
Vuori has not been acquired; instead, it has received significant investments:

- In August 2019, Norwest Venture Partners invested $45 million. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2019/08/15/southern-california-athletic-brand-vuori-raises-45-million-from-norwest-venture-partners/))

- In October 2021, SoftBank Vision Fund 2 invested $400 million, valuing Vuori at $4 billion. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20211013005305/en/Vuori-Receives-400-Million-Investment-and-4B-Valuation-from-SoftBank-Vision-Fund-2))

- In November 2024, General Atlantic and Stripes led an $825 million investment, raising the valuation to $5.5 billion. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241108508233/en/Vuori-Announces-825-Million-Investment-Led-by-General-Atlantic-and-Stripes/))

Business Category: Prana Yoga Center offers yoga services and sells back care products online. ([bbb.org](https://www.bbb.org/us/ca/la-jolla/profile/yoga-supplies/prana-yoga-center-1126-17001488))
Ownership Details: Prana Yoga Center, founded in 2001 by Gerhard and Alexandra Gessner, is now owned by the Quality Longevity Foundation, a 501(c) non-profit organization dedicated to enhancing health and longevity. ([prana-yoga.com](https://prana-yoga.com/about/))
Headquarters: Prana Yoga Center is located in La Jolla, California. ([prana-yoga.com](https://prana-yoga.com/contact-us/))
Main Manufacturing: Prana manufactures its yoga clothing in several countries, including China, India, Vietnam, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, and Thailand. The company emphasizes sustainability and ethical practices, with many of its factories being Fair Trade Certified. ([worldchangerco.com](https://www.worldchangerco.com/activewear2))
Environmental & Sustainability Policies: Prana, a yoga apparel brand, integrates sustainability by using eco-friendly materials like organic cotton, hemp, and recycled polyester, and by partnering with Fair Trade Certified™ manufacturers. ([yogabasics.com](https://www.yogabasics.com/connect/yoga-blog/eco-friendly-yoga-fashion/)) They have also eliminated plastic from their consumer packaging and launched the Responsible Packaging Movement to promote sustainable packaging practices across the industry. ([sustainablebrands.com](https://sustainablebrands.com/read/prana-s-responsible-packaging-movement-aims-to-foster-sea-change-in-fashion))
Acquisition History: Prana, founded in 1992, was acquired by Liz Claiborne Inc. in 2005 for $34.4 million, sold back to its original owners in 2008 for $36.5 million, and purchased by Columbia Sportswear in 2014 for $190 million. ([sagellc.com](https://sagellc.com/news/2005/liz-clairborne-acquires-prana/), [apparelnews.net](https://www.apparelnews.net/news/2014/jun/02/columbia-sportswear-completes-acquisition-prana-ap/))

Business Category: Stio is a mountain lifestyle brand specializing in outdoor apparel and accessories, including ski and snowboard clothing designed for performance and style. Main Manufacturing: Stio designs, develops, and tests its ski apparel in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, but manufactures products in the USA, Canada, China, El Salvador, and Vietnam. ([postvines.com](https://postvines.com/where-are-stio-products-manufactured/))
Environmental & Sustainability Policies: Stio, a mountain lifestyle brand, is committed to eliminating per- and polyfluorinated chemicals (PFAS) from its technical apparel, footwear, and accessories by 2025, with over half of its Fall/Winter 2023-24 collection already PFAS-free. ([textileworld.com](https://www.textileworld.com/textile-world/knitting-apparel/2023/10/jackson-hole-based-technical-outerwear-brand-stio-commits-to-removing-pfas-by-2025/)) Additionally, Stio aims to use 75% preferred materials by 2025, having increased usage to 57% in 2023, and has been Climate Neutral-certified since 2020. Acquisition History: In August 2014, Stio received a $5.9 million investment led by Sandbridge Capital, with KarpReilly converting existing debt to equity. ([shop-eat-surf-outdoor.com](https://shop-eat-surf-outdoor.com/press-releases/stio-raises-20m-in-capital-to-accelerate-omnichannel-growth/131028/))

Business Category: Smith Optics specializes in high-performance eyewear and helmets for skiing and cycling, offering products like snow goggles, ski helmets, cycling sunglasses, and bike helmets. ([smithoptics.com](https://www.smithoptics.com/en_US/c/sunglasses-activity-cycling/), [rosebikes.com](https://www.rosebikes.com/brands/smith-optics))
Financials: Smith Optics, a subsidiary of Safilo Group S.p.A., reported a return to growth in its sports business during Q4 2024, driven by direct-to-consumer sales and a strong start to the 2025 ski season. ([sgbonline.com](https://sgbonline.com/exec-smith-optics-returned-to-growth-in-2024-q4/)) In 2022, the company experienced a 22% revenue increase attributed to its ChromaPop lens innovation. ([pmarketresearch.com](https://pmarketresearch.com/hc/mens-ski-goggles-market/))
Ownership Details: Subsidiary of Safilo Group
Main Manufacturing: Smith Optics manufactures its ski goggles and sunglasses at its facility in Clearfield, Utah. ([blog.smithoptics.com](https://blog.smithoptics.com/b/a-look-inside-smith-mfg/)) However, their helmets are produced in China. ([americanalternative.wordpress.com](https://americanalternative.wordpress.com/tag/smith-optics/))
Environmental & Sustainability Policies:
Smith Optics has implemented several environmental and sustainability initiatives:

- In Fall 2022, they eliminated plastic waste from their winter sports and eyewear packaging, removing plastic windows and polybags, and partnered with gogglesoc™ to provide lens protectors made from recycled water bottles. ([blog.smithoptics.com](https://blog.smithoptics.com/b/moving-towards-zero-plastic-waste/))

- Their CORE Collection features sunglasses made from 100% post-consumer plastic, including recycled water bottles, with bio-based polarized lenses and recycled PET frames. ([deal.town](https://deal.town/smith-optics/explore-core-for-the-planet-FKA87E6Y2))

- The Evolve program, launched in 2009, utilizes bio-based or recycled materials across all product categories to reduce environmental impact. ([atlasskico.com](https://atlasskico.com/partners/smith-optics))

- Smith's Pivlock V2 Max sunglasses incorporate Evolve frame material, made from over 53% bio-based Rilsan Clear, offering lightweight and durable frames. ([actionsportoptics.com](https://actionsportoptics.com/blog/c/eyewear-tech/page/20/))

- In 2021, Smith employees participated in Earth Day clean-up events, collaborating with non-profits to maintain local trails and parks. ([blog.smithoptics.com](https://blog.smithoptics.com/b/smith-x-earth-day-clean-up/))

- The company offers fitness benefits, including locker rooms and gear storage, and encourages outdoor activities like skiing and biking, promoting a healthy lifestyle among employees. ([outsideonline.com](https://www.outsideonline.com/outdoor-gear/clothing-apparel/4-smith-optics/))

- Smith provides flexible hours, allowing employees to adjust schedules for outdoor activities, fostering a work-life balance that aligns with their outdoor-focused culture. ([outsideonline.com](https://www.outsideonline.com/outdoor-gear/clothing-apparel/4-smith-optics/))

- The company supports various community service initiatives, including partnerships with local and national organizations, reflecting their commitment to social responsibility. ([outsideonline.com](https://www.outsideonline.com/outdoor-gear/clothing-apparel/4-smith-optics/))

- Smith's green initiatives include using recycled materials in goggles, FSC-certified paper for packaging, and offering commuting benefits like bus passes and bike racks. ([outsideonline.com](https://www.outsideonline.com/outdoor-gear/clothing-apparel/4-smith-optics/))

- Employees receive recreation allowances and have access to facilities like locker rooms and gear rooms, promoting active engagement in outdoor sports. ([outsideonline.com](https://www.outsideonline.com/outdoor-gear/clothing-apparel/4-smith-optics/))
Acquisition History: Smith Optics was acquired by Safilo Group in 1996. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Safilo))

Business Category: GSI Outdoors specializes in manufacturing camping cookware and accessories, offering products such as camp stoves, cooksets, and tableware designed for outdoor use. ([bbb.org](https://www.bbb.org/us/wa/spokane-vly/profile/camping-equipment/gsi-outdoors-1296-15002012))
Financials: GSI Outdoors, founded in 1985 and based in Spokane, Washington, specializes in outdoor cookware and dining products. In May 2022, Pelican International acquired a majority stake in the company. ([sgbonline.com](https://sgbonline.com/pelican-international-acquires-gsi-outdoors/)) Estimates of GSI Outdoors' annual revenue vary, with figures ranging from $2.45 million to $7.1 million, and employee counts between 20 and 38. ([manta.com](https://www.manta.com/c/mmghzbt/gsi-outdoors), [compworth.com](https://compworth.com/company/gsi-outdoors))
Ownership Details: In May 2022, Pelican International Inc. acquired a majority stake in GSI Outdoors, a Spokane, Washington-based company specializing in outdoor cooking and hydration products. ([sgbonline.com](https://sgbonline.com/pelican-international-acquires-gsi-outdoors/)) Co-founders Don and Ian Scott continue to lead the company under Pelican's ownership. ([gearjunkie.com](https://gearjunkie.com/camping/pelican-international-acquires-gsi-outdoors))
Main Manufacturing: GSI Outdoors manufactures its camping products in Taiwan, Vietnam, and China, leveraging each country's expertise in producing high-quality outdoor gear. ([godsempires.com](https://godsempires.com/where-is-gsi-made-l-en/))
Environmental & Sustainability Policies:
GSI Outdoors is committed to environmental sustainability through several key initiatives:

- **PFAS Phase-Out**: The company has replaced PFAS-based non-stick coatings with clean ceramic alternatives and collaborates with suppliers to develop PFAS-free materials and production processes. ([gsioutdoors.zendesk.com](https://gsioutdoors.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35727065323533-PFAS-Phase-Out-Key-Steps-for-GSI-Outdoors))

- **Responsible Packaging**: GSI Outdoors joined Prana’s Responsible Packaging Movement, committing to use non-plastic packaging for new product lines starting in 2021 and phasing out plastic packaging in existing lines by 2023. ([outdoorsportswire.com](https://www.outdoorsportswire.com/gsi-outdoors-joins-pranas-responsible-packaging-movement/))

- **Supply Chain Responsibility**: The company ensures best work practices across its supply chain, with factories conforming to strict environmental and social mandates, and products undergoing extensive testing to comply with safety standards. ([gsioutdoors.zendesk.com](https://gsioutdoors.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360049234051-Our-Supply-Chain-Responsibility))

- **Carbon Offset Partnership**: GSI Outdoors partnered with SeaTrees to plant mangrove trees, aiming to counterbalance carbon emissions by sequestering CO₂ efficiently. ([outdoorsportswire.com](https://www.outdoorsportswire.com/gsi-outdoors-expands-partnership-with-nonprofit-organization-seatrees/))

- **Industry Collaboration**: As a member of the Outdoor Industry Association's Clean Chemistry and Materials Coalition, GSI Outdoors follows a scalable action plan to eliminate harmful chemicals and enhance supply chain transparency. ([outdoorindustry.org](https://outdoorindustry.org/ccmc-progress-report/gsi-outdoors-llc-2024/))
Acquisition History: In January 2006, GSI Outdoors acquired Thrive, a startup specializing in outdoor cutlery, and appointed Thrive's former owner, Mike Glavin, as director of sales and marketing. ([sgbonline.com](https://sgbonline.com/gsi-outdoors-acquires-thrive/)) In May 2022, Pelican International acquired a majority stake in GSI Outdoors to diversify its offerings in the outdoor equipment sector. ([sgbonline.com](https://sgbonline.com/pelican-international-acquires-gsi-outdoors/))

Business Category: Dakine specializes in manufacturing and selling high-quality products for surfing and snowboarding, including surf leashes, surf pads, surfboard travel bags, wetsuits, ski and snowboard gloves, jackets, pants, and travel bags. ([dakine-europe.com](https://dakine-europe.com/eu_en/sport-140))
Financials: As of January 2025, Dakine's annual revenue is estimated between $25 million and $50 million. ([similarweb.com](https://www.similarweb.com/company/dakine.com/)) Specific financial data for its surfing and snowboarding segments is not publicly available.
Ownership Details: Owned by Altamont Capital
Main Manufacturing: Dakine's surfing and snowboarding products are manufactured overseas. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dakine))
Environmental & Sustainability Policies: Dakine has partnered with 1% for the Planet, pledging to donate 1% of gross e-commerce sales annually to environmental nonprofits. ([shop-eat-surf.com](https://shop-eat-surf.com/2022/05/dakine-kicks-off-partnership-with-1-for-the-planet/)) They utilize 100% recycled and bluesign®-approved materials in their snow, bike, and travel bag lines, and employ biodegradable Friendly Foam in surf traction products. ([dakine-europe.com](https://dakine-europe.com/ch_en/sustainability))
Acquisition History:
Dakine, founded in 1979, has undergone several ownership changes:

- In 2008, Billabong International Ltd. acquired Dakine for approximately $100 million. ([sgbonline.com](https://sgbonline.com/billabong-adds-dakine-to-action-sports-empire/))

- In 2013, Altamont Capital Partners purchased Dakine from Billabong. ([prnewswire.com](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/altamont-capital-partners-agrees-to-acquire-dakine-215627261.html))

- In 2018, Marquee Brands LLC acquired Dakine and entered into a long-term license agreement with JR286 for its operations. ([businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20181205005450/en/Marquee-Brands-Acquires-Dakine%C2%AE))
